meta.verifiedNote: PAGASA Tropical Cyclone Bulletin No. 6 (bulletin_inday.pdf). Current AND forecast positions are exact PAGASA coordinates from the bulletin's Track & Intensity Forecast table.

current.name: INDAY
current.positionRef: 925 km East of Northern Luzon (inside PAR)
current.outlook: INDAY has weakened into a typhoon. It moves northwestward over the Philippine Sea and may pass closest to Extreme Northern Luzon between Fri 10 Jul evening and Sat 11 Jul morning. It then heads toward the southern islands of Japan and may make landfall or pass over the northern coast of Taiwan; exits PAR Sat 11 Jul; forecast landfall over eastern mainland China (vicinity of Zhejiang) Sun 12 Jul, weakening to a severe tropical storm inland. PH landfall unlikely.

signal1: Northern Abra (Tineg, Lagayan, Danglas, San Juan, Lagangilang, Licuan-Baay, Malibcong, Lacub, Daguioman, Dolores, La Paz)
tcws.note: Signal No. 1 = 39-61 km/h winds possible within 36 hrs; minimal to minor threat. Full municipality list per PAGASA Bulletin No. 6.

gusts.today: Most of the country (today until Saturday, 11 Jul)

Zambales, Bataan, Pampanga, Bulacan, Metro Manila, Cavite, Batangas, Oriental Mindoro, Romblon, Palawan, Aklan, Iloilo, Guimaras, Negros Occ., Negros Or., Zamboanga del Sur, Lanao del Norte

rainfall.note: Per PAGASA Weather Advisory No. 11 (5:00 AM, 09 Jul) — TC INDAY + Southwest Monsoon. Floods and landslides are likely.

marine.galeWarning: Gale Warning No. 3 in effect (5:00 AM) — northern & eastern seaboards of Luzon, and the eastern seaboard of Visayas.

waves.where: Seaboards of Batanes; northern & eastern seaboards of Babuyan Islands; northeastern seaboard of mainland Cagayan
Remaining seaboards of mainland Cagayan and Babuyan Islands; seaboard of Isabela

Seaboards of Aurora and Camarines Norte; northern seaboard of Camarines Sur; northern & eastern seaboards of Polillo Islands and Catanduanes; eastern seaboard of Northern Samar; northeastern seaboard of Eastern Samar

marine.advice: Sea travel is risky for all types/tonnage of vessels in the affected seaboards. All mariners must remain in port or seek shelter/safe harbor until winds and waves subside.

satellite.note: Satellite imagery is visual context only. Cloud position/appearance must not be used to infer storm movement - the official center and track come from PAGASA.
